name: Jabbo Smith
abstract: Jabbo Smith (born Cladys Smith; December 24, 1908 – January 16, 1991) was an American jazz musician, known for his virtuoso playing on the trumpet.

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Oakley Dance
about_quotes.evidence: Jabbo came in and was in the band, and Scorsoothy, and all those musicians that I’m talking about, Jess and so on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Helen Oakley Dance interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Michael White
about_quotes.evidence: But that song, which some people say sounds like klezmer to them originally was influenced by a Jabbo Smith song.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael White interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 10, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: We had Jabbo Smith, the young Jabbo Smith, in his teens... in the band that I worked with
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Waters interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 1, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Zilner T. Randolph
about_quotes.evidence: You were talking about the Sunset All Stars. And you said that Jabbo Smith was in there... [INTERVIEWEE] Preston Jackson was on trombone... And Joe got a kick out of that. [INTERVIEWER] Well, was Jabbo Smith featured a lot with that band? [INTERVIEWEE] Yes. He stayed there a while.

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: incidentally, these were Jenkins boys that worked in this cabaret... Jabbo Smith... came out of Fred Cooley... That band traveled all over the world... And except Willy Gant the rest of us were geechees out of South Carolina and from Jenkins Orphanage

about_quotes.speaker: J. C. Higginbotham
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Jabbo Smith only in the context of listing musicians from the Jenkins Orphanage ('Jabberwocky, he came from there... That's about all I remember'), without describing a personal interaction or professional collaboration.
